title: Grand Rapids Crime, 1985–2026 — How It Rose, Fell, and Moved | Data Map
description:
Every reported crime in Grand Rapids, Michigan — mapped and counted from 1985 to 2026.

This is a fully sourced, reproducible data visualization. No incident is invented and no dot is a fabricated location: figures come straight from the FBI UCR (1985–2022) and the Grand Rapids Police Department's NIBRS open data (2023–2026), aggregated by police beat. Where the data has no coordinates, dots show density within a beat — how many, never exactly where. Coverage and caveats are shown on screen.

▶ CHAPTERS
0:00  Hook — crime halved since 1985
0:08  What you're about to see
0:22  Chapter 1 — the long arc, 1985–2025
2:30  2023: the map comes alive
2:43  Chapter 2 — neighborhood by neighborhood
4:52  The busiest & safest neighborhoods
5:18  Sources & credits

▶ DATA SOURCES
• Grand Rapids Police Department crime data (2023–), City of Grand Rapids ArcGIS Hub
• FBI Uniform Crime Reporting / Crime Data Explorer (1985–2022), ORI MI4143600
• GRPD Service Area (beat) polygons & City of Grand Rapids Neighborhood Areas
Used under the City of Grand Rapids GIS Data Access & Use Constraint Agreement (provided "as is").

▶ MADE BY AN AI — FULLY TRANSPARENT
This video was produced end-to-end by Claude (Anthropic's AI): it located and fetched the official source data, built and validated the data pipeline, verified every on-screen figure against the sources, designed and rendered the visuals, and generated the music. No human edited the numbers. Every step — code, data, and design decisions — is public and reproducible in the repository linked below. If you find an error, open an issue; corrections are part of the record.

▶ CODE, DATA & FULL SOURCES (reproduce every number)
https://github.com/hadi-nayebi/crime-cartography/tree/main/videos/grand-rapids-mi

Music generated with Stable Audio Open (Stability AI).

If this was worth a look, please Like, Subscribe, and Share it with someone from Grand Rapids.

#GrandRapids #CrimeData #DataVisualization #OpenData
